LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PATRICIA LOUIE'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE TO RECOMMEND DISPOSITION OF PROPERTY IN PARTITION DISPUTE. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED, MOTION UNNECESSARY AS PARTIES AGREE PROPERTY SHOULD BE SOLD. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER (WITHIN 15 DAYS) REGARDING LISTING AGENT. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REALTOR, THEY CAN BRING A MOTION TO APPOINT A REALTOR. MR. HAAS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO; REPORTER: GORDON AIAVAO, CSR #11216

Notic And Motion For Appointment Of Referee To Recommend Disposition Of Property In Partition Dispute
Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PATRICIA LOUIE'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE TO RECOMMEND DISPOSITION OF PROPERTY IN PARTITION DISPUTE. Appearances: Gerard Launay (no phone number provided) for defendant and responding party; Edward Haas (no card provided) for plaintiff and moving party. Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied, motion unnecessary as parties agree property should be sold. Parties to meet and confer (within 15 days) regarding listing agent. If the parties cannot agree on a realtor, they can bring a motion to appoint a realtor. Mr. Haas to submit a proposed form of order. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216
